flashcards.front: What is the core principle of Forensic Soundness?
flashcards.back: The assurance that digital evidence has been collected, examined, and stored in a manner that preserves its integrity and prevents any alteration.
A bit-for-bit copy of the entire physical storage medium, including deleted files and unallocated space.

flashcards.front: What is the main limitation of Logical Acquisition?
flashcards.back: It only recovers active, allocated files and cannot retrieve deleted data or unallocated space.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of a Faraday bag in mobile device seizure?
flashcards.back: To block all radio frequency signals (cellular, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth), preventing data alteration or remote wipe.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between JTAG and Chip-Off acquisition?
flashcards.back: JTAG interfaces with the memory chip while it is still soldered to the board. Chip-Off involves physically de-soldering and removing the memory chip to read it externally.
